g.text: ⑤a fsync(database_fd) — All DB Writes Durable
0x14 | db_page_cnt | N at txn start| 4 B
Total header: 512 B (padded to sector boundary)
② Before EACH Page Modification — Journal Original
GUARANTEE: original page data is DURABLE on persistent storage
INVARIANT: this fsync MUST complete before ANY write to .db file
Crash AFTER this point → journal holds all originals → recovery works
Crash BEFORE this point → .db untouched → no recovery needed
④ Write Modified Pages to Database File
status | COMPLETE AND DURABLE | safety net
Journal is the recovery anchor during this phase
page 117 | OLD DATA | not yet written
Exact state varies per crash timing
After return: every modified page is on persistent storage
Stops at first checksum fail — torn journal write is safe
Result: database restored to consistent pre-txn state
Purple — crash recovery path on restart
CRITICAL: pread from .db file, NOT buffer pool
Buffer pool may hold in-memory modifications already
STILL UNTOUCHED — journal is safety net
Pages written in LRU eviction order or explicit flush
Write order within this step does NOT matter for correctness
COMMITTED — all modifications durable and consistent
Recovery: restore ALL journaled pages from journal
Crash during unlink → recovery rolls back
first write → journal created & header fsynced
